title: Origanum bracteatum
intro: Origanum bracteatum, commonly known as Syrian marjoram, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Syrian marjoram include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and essential oils, particularly carvacrol and thymol, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Syrian marjoram include teas, infusions, and essential oil extracts, which can be used to support respiratory health, alleviate digestive issues, and provide antioxidant benefits.
